text: Reproduces the symptom (actually runs the report) and examines the real data and output before proposing or making any change.

Localizes the failing layer by inspecting what crosses the loader/report boundary (printing, logging, or directly examining the parsed values), identifying the loader's number parsing as the failure point with evidence — not by guessing from the report's arithmetic.
States an explicit root-cause hypothesis (decimal-comma prices mis-parsed into hundredfold values) before changing code, and tests it minimally rather than changing several things at once.
The fix lands at the root cause — the loader's price parsing — not as a compensating patch where the symptom shows (no dividing totals by 100, no special-casing in the report).
A failing test reproducing the mis-parse was added before the fix and shown passing after, and the report was re-run to confirm the symptom is gone.
